model.description: MiMo • Flash-tier model configured through a custom endpoint for fast inference and lightweight agent tasks.

pick.rationale: UCLA holds a clear edge in advanced metrics, ranking 24th in Torvik and 27th in KenPom compared to UCF's 58th and 54th, with a stronger adjusted offensive efficiency and a defense that should contain the Knights' attack. While UCF has shown resilience in close games and plays in the tough Big 12, UCLA's superior talent, experience, and recent form—including a dominant sweep of USC—make them the more reliable pick. The underdog's path relies on hot three-point shooting and forcing turnovers, but UCLA's balanced attack and defensive prowess should control the tempo and secure the win.

reasoningStep.title: Holistic matchup analysis for UCLA vs UCF
reasoningStep.summary: Evaluated team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, and upset risk using live tools and prior bracket context.
reasoningStep.evidence: UCLA ranks 24th in Torvik and 27th in KenPom with elite offensive efficiency (adjoe 124.5) and solid defense (adjde 101.7), while UCF is 58th/54th with weaker defense (adjde 105.7). Injury reports indicate UCLA's key players are healthy. ESPN analytics give UCLA a 72.2% win probability. Prior picks show UCLA's path through the bracket is feasible, and UCF's upset potential is limited without a major shooting night.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

The tenth-seeded UCF Knights will face the seventh-seeded UCLA Bruins in the first round of the NCAA Tournament. UCF coach Johnny Dawkins and UCLA coach Mick Cronin have previously faced each other six times. UCLA's leading scorer, Tyler Bilodeau, is expected to play after suffering a mild knee strain in the Big Ten tournament. ORLANDO — The East region is loaded with college basketball's traditional bluebloods, including the UCF Knights ' first-round opponent, 11-time national champion UCLA . Tenth-seeded UCF (21-11) will square off with the Bruins (23-11) in an opening-round NCAA Tournament matchup at 7:25 p.m. on March 20 at Xfinity Mobile Arena in Philadelphia. By contrast, UCF will make just its sixth March Madness appearance since joining Division I in 1984-85. Johnny Dawkins coached the Knights to their last trip to the Big Dance back in 2019. modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.

Tournament year: 2026
Current game id: r64-east-7
Current game label: East 7/10
Round: Round of 64
Resolved teams: UCLA (ucla) vs UCF (ucf)
Prior committed picks: 10
